   119→
   120→## ⚠️ Critical Development Priorities
   121→
   122→> **These principles are NON-NEGOTIABLE.** Every decision, feature, and line of code must align with these priorities. When in conflict, priorities higher in the list take precedence.
   123→
   124→### Priority Hierarchy (In Order)
   125→
   126→#### 1. 🔒 SECURITY (Non-Negotiable)
   127→- **Defense in depth** - Multiple layers of protection, never rely on single control
   128→- **Never trust user input** - Validate, sanitize, escape at every boundary
   129→- **Secure by default** - New features must be secure out of the box
   130→- **Zero tolerance for secrets in code** - No API keys, tokens, passwords in source
   131→- **OWASP Top 10 awareness** - Actively prevent XSS, injection, CSRF, auth issues
   132→- **Data minimization** - Only collect/store what's absolutely necessary
   133→- **Audit logging** - Track security-relevant actions for forensics
   134→
   135→#### 2. 🏗️ FOUNDATIONAL (Build Right)
   136→- **Solid architecture first** - No shortcuts that create tech debt
   137→- **Single source of truth** - One authoritative location for every piece of data
   138→- **Clean integration** - New features must integrate with existing systems cleanly
   139→- **Backward compatibility** - Don't break existing functionality without migration path
   140→- **Database integrity** - Proper constraints, indexes, foreign keys, cascades
   141→
   142→#### 3. 🛡️ STABILITY (Never Crash)
   143→- **Zero tolerance for crashes** - Comprehensive error handling everywhere
   144→- **No data loss** - Every user action either succeeds completely or fails safely
   145→- **Graceful degradation** - Features fail gracefully when dependencies unavailable
   146→- **Test critical paths** - Auth, payments, data mutations must have test coverage
   147→- **Defensive programming** - Assume inputs are malformed, APIs are down, state is corrupted
   148→
   149→#### 4. ✨ UX/UI (User Delight)
   150→- **Speed is a feature** - Target <100ms perceived interaction latency
   151→- **Mobile-first responsive** - Design for mobile, enhance for desktop
   152→- **Intuitive flows** - Users should never wonder "what do I do next?"
   153→- **Accessibility required** - WCAG 2.1 AA compliance, keyboard nav, screen readers
   154→- **Consistent design language** - Miami Vice theme applied uniformly
   155→- **Feedback on every action** - Loading states, success messages, error explanations
   156→- **Forgiveness** - Easy undo, confirmation for destructive actions
   157→
   158→#### 5. 🎯 LEAN (Ship Minimal)
   159→- **MVP first** - Ship the minimum viable solution, iterate based on feedback
   160→- **No feature creep** - If it's not solving a real user problem, don't build it
   161→- **Question every dependency** - Each package adds risk, size, and maintenance burden
   162→- **Simpler is better** - Fewer abstractions, less code, easier maintenance
   163→- **Delete before adding** - Remove unused code before adding new features
   164→
   165→#### 6. 🧹 CLEAN (Readable Code)
   166→- **Readable > clever** - Anyone should understand code without deep context
   167→- **Consistent naming** - Follow established conventions throughout
   168→- **Single responsibility** - Each function/component does one thing well
   169→- **No dead code** - Remove unused imports, functions, files immediately
   170→- **Self-documenting** - Code explains "what", comments explain "why"
   171→
   172→#### 7. 📈 RETENTION (Bring Users Back)
   173→- **Valuable data** - Help users track stats they can't get elsewhere
   174→- **Progress visualization** - Show improvement over time
   175→- **Social features** - Playing partners, comparisons, friendly competition
   176→- **Low friction** - One-tap round logging, smart defaults, remember preferences
   177→- **Notifications done right** - Valuable reminders, never spam
   178→
   179→#### 8. ⚡ EFFICIENT + OPTIMIZED (Performance)
   180→- **Measure first** - Profile before optimizing, no premature optimization
   181→- **Performance budgets** - Set and enforce bundle size limits
   182→- **Core Web Vitals** - LCP < 2.5s, FID < 100ms, CLS < 0.1
   183→- **Database optimization** - Proper indexes, query analysis, N+1 prevention
   184→- **Caching strategy** - Cache at every layer (CDN, API, database, client)
   185→- **Lazy loading** - Load code and data only when needed
   186→
   187→#### 9. 🔄 UPDATED + KNOWLEDGEABLE (Stay Current)
   188→- **Current dependencies** - Regular updates, security patches applied promptly
   189→- **Modern patterns** - Use current best practices, avoid deprecated approaches
   190→- **Understand before changing** - Read existing code thoroughly before modifying
   191→- **Know the "why"** - Understand reasoning behind architectural decisions
   192→- **Documentation current** - CLAUDE.md and docs updated with every change
   193→
   194→#### 10. 🏌️ INDUSTRY-FAMILIAR (Golf Domain Expertise)
   195→- **Handicap systems** - GHIN, WHS, course/slope ratings, handicap index calculation
   196→- **Golf terminology** - Correct use of GIR, FIR, putts per round, scoring terms
   197→- **Course data** - Par, rating, slope, tee boxes, layouts
   198→- **Tournament formats** - Stroke play, match play, Stableford, scramble
   199→- **Competitor awareness** - Know what GHIN, Golf Pad, 18Birdies offer
   200→
   201→---
   202→
   203→### Golden Rules (Never Break)
   204→
   205→1. **CSV is source of truth** - Never edit JSON in `/data/` directly
   206→2. **Always run `npx prisma generate`** after schema.prisma changes
   207→3. **Protected routes need `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard)`**
   208→4. **Admin routes need `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard, AdminGuard)`**
   209→5. **All inputs validated** via class-validator DTOs
   210→6. **Never commit secrets** - Check `.env`, API keys, tokens
   211→7. **Test before deploy** - No untested code reaches production
   212→8. **Mobile-first** - Every feature must work on mobile devices
   213→
   214→### Before Completing ANY Task
   215→
   216→- [ ] **Security:** No vulnerabilities introduced (injection, XSS, auth bypass)
   217→- [ ] **Stability:** Error handling for all failure modes
   218→- [ ] **UX:** Works on mobile, accessible, has loading/error states
   219→- [ ] **Lean:** No unnecessary code, dependencies, or features added
   220→- [ ] **Clean:** Follows existing patterns, readable, no dead code
   221→- [ ] **Tests:** Critical paths have test coverage
   222→- [ ] **Backend:** `npm run test` passes
   223→- [ ] **Frontend:** `npm run build` passes, `npm run check:data` passes
   224→- [ ] **Docs:** CLAUDE.md updated if patterns changed

   245→## Architecture Overview
   246→
   247→### Frontend Data Flow
   248→
   249→```
   250→CSV files (source of truth)     JSON (build artifact)       Browser
   251→golfers/golfers.csv      -->    data/golfers.json     -->   fetch + render
   252→leagues/leagues.csv      -->    data/leagues.json

   256→### Backend Module Structure
   257→
   258→```
   259→src/
   260→├── auth/           # Google OAuth + JWT (cookie: lg_jwt)
   261→├── profile/        # GET/PUT /v1/me
   262→├── rounds/         # CRUD /v1/rounds
   263→├── courses/        # Course management
   264→├── prisma/         # Database service (global module)
   265→└── audit-log/      # Action logging
   266→```
   267→
   268→### Database Models
   269→
   270→| Model | Description |
   271→|-------|-------------|
   272→| `User` | OAuth identity (Google), 1:1 with Player |
   273→| `Player` | Golf profile (handicap, GHIN), has many Rounds |
   274→| `Round` | Golf rounds with stats |
   275→| `Course` | Course data with API integration |
   276→| `RoundLink` | Playing partner linking |
   277→| `Role/UserRole` | RBAC |
   278→| `AuditLog` | Action tracking |
   279→
   280→### Authentication Flow
   281→
   282→1. Frontend → `GET /auth/google` → Google consent
   283→2. Google → Backend callback with code
   284→3. Backend creates/updates User + Player
   285→4. Backend signs JWT, sets `lg_jwt` HttpOnly cookie
   286→5. Backend redirects to frontend success page
   287→6. Frontend calls `/auth/session` to confirm

# CLAUDE.md - Lazy Golfing Platform

> AI assistant guidelines for the LazyGolfing monorepo

**Last Updated:** 2025-12-06

---

## Quick Reference

### Most-Used Commands

```bash
# Frontend (lzg.home/)
npm run dev                     # Development server (localhost:3000)
npm run build                   # Production build (static export)
npm run lint                    # ESLint checks
npm run build:data              # CSV → JSON (legacy /alpha site)
npm run check:data              # Verify JSON is current

# Backend (lzg.backend/) - Run on VM (192.168.1.40)
npm run start:dev               # Development server with hot-reload
npm run test                    # Unit tests
npx prisma generate             # REQUIRED after schema.prisma changes
npx prisma migrate dev --name <name>  # Create migration
npx prisma studio               # Database GUI (192.168.1.40:5555)

# Production
pm2 logs lg-api --lines 100     # View backend logs
pm2 restart lg-api              # Restart after deploy
```

### Key API Endpoints

| Endpoint | Auth | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| `GET /auth/google` | None | Start OAuth flow |
| `GET /auth/session` | None | Check login status |
| `POST /auth/logout` | CSRF | Clear session |
| `GET /v1/me` | JWT+CSRF | Get user profile |
| `PUT /v1/me` | JWT+CSRF | Update profile |
| `GET /v1/rounds` | JWT+CSRF | List rounds (paginated) |
| `POST /v1/rounds` | JWT+CSRF | Create round |
| `GET /v1/courses/search` | None | Search courses |

See [API Reference](./docs/architecture/API_REFERENCE.md) for complete endpoint documentation.

### Test Commands

```bash
# Test API connectivity
curl https://api.lazygolfing.com/
curl https://api.lazygolfing.com/auth/session
```

---

## Repository Structure

```
Lazy Golfing/
├── CLAUDE.md                    # This file - root AI instructions
├── docs/                        # Centralized documentation
│   ├── plans/                   # Product roadmap, feature roadmap
│   ├── todo/                    # Task tracking
│   ├── technical-debt/          # Code quality issues
│   ├── design/                  # Design system documentation
│   └── architecture/            # System design docs
├── media/                       # Brand assets (not deployed)
│   ├── logos/                   # Official logos and variants
│   ├── kikman/                  # Kikman sub-brand assets
│   └── archive/                 # Historical planning screenshots
├── lzg.home/                    # Frontend (Next.js 15 + React 19)
│   ├── CLAUDE.md               # Frontend-specific AI instructions
│   ├── src/app/                # Next.js App Router pages
│   ├── src/components/         # React components + shadcn/ui
│   ├── src/lib/theme.ts        # Centralized color config
│   └── public/alpha/           # Legacy static site (preserved)
└── lzg.backend/                 # Backend (NestJS API)
    ├── CLAUDE.md               # Backend-specific AI instructions
    └── README.md               # Backend documentation
```

### Documentation Hierarchy

| File | Purpose | When to Read |
|------|---------|--------------|
| `/CLAUDE.md` | Quick reference, critical rules | Always |
| `/docs/` | Detailed documentation | Planning, research |
| `/lzg.home/CLAUDE.md` | Next.js/React patterns | Working on frontend |
| `/lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md` | API/database details | Working on backend |

---

## Development Setup

### Environment

- **Code editing:** Windows machine (this repo)
- **Backend runtime:** Ubuntu VM at `192.168.1.40`
- **Production API:** https://api.lazygolfing.com
- **Production Frontend:** https://lazygolfing.com

### First-Time Setup

```bash
# Frontend
cd lzg.home && npm ci && npm run build:data

# Backend (on VM)
cd lzg.backend && npm ci
cp .env.example .env  # Configure environment
npx prisma generate
npx prisma migrate dev
npm run start:dev
```

---

## ⚠️ Critical Development Priorities

> **These principles are NON-NEGOTIABLE.** Every decision, feature, and line of code must align with these priorities. When in conflict, priorities higher in the list take precedence.

### Priority Hierarchy (In Order)

#### 1. 🔒 SECURITY (Non-Negotiable)
- **Defense in depth** - Multiple layers of protection, never rely on single control
- **Never trust user input** - Validate, sanitize, escape at every boundary
- **Secure by default** - New features must be secure out of the box
- **Zero tolerance for secrets in code** - No API keys, tokens, passwords in source
- **OWASP Top 10 awareness** - Actively prevent XSS, injection, CSRF, auth issues
- **Data minimization** - Only collect/store what's absolutely necessary
- **Audit logging** - Track security-relevant actions for forensics

#### 2. 🏗️ FOUNDATIONAL (Build Right)
- **Solid architecture first** - No shortcuts that create tech debt
- **Single source of truth** - One authoritative location for every piece of data
- **Clean integration** - New features must integrate with existing systems cleanly
- **Backward compatibility** - Don't break existing functionality without migration path
- **Database integrity** - Proper constraints, indexes, foreign keys, cascades

#### 3. 🛡️ STABILITY (Never Crash)
- **Zero tolerance for crashes** - Comprehensive error handling everywhere
- **No data loss** - Every user action either succeeds completely or fails safely
- **Graceful degradation** - Features fail gracefully when dependencies unavailable
- **Test critical paths** - Auth, payments, data mutations must have test coverage
- **Defensive programming** - Assume inputs are malformed, APIs are down, state is corrupted

#### 4. ✨ UX/UI (User Delight)
- **Speed is a feature** - Target <100ms perceived interaction latency
- **Mobile-first responsive** - Design for mobile, enhance for desktop
- **Intuitive flows** - Users should never wonder "what do I do next?"
- **Accessibility required** - WCAG 2.1 AA compliance, keyboard nav, screen readers
- **Consistent design language** - Miami Vice theme applied uniformly
- **Feedback on every action** - Loading states, success messages, error explanations
- **Forgiveness** - Easy undo, confirmation for destructive actions

#### 5. 🎯 LEAN (Ship Minimal)
- **MVP first** - Ship the minimum viable solution, iterate based on feedback
- **No feature creep** - If it's not solving a real user problem, don't build it
- **Question every dependency** - Each package adds risk, size, and maintenance burden
- **Simpler is better** - Fewer abstractions, less code, easier maintenance
- **Delete before adding** - Remove unused code before adding new features

#### 6. 🧹 CLEAN (Readable Code)
- **Readable > clever** - Anyone should understand code without deep context
- **Consistent naming** - Follow established conventions throughout
- **Single responsibility** - Each function/component does one thing well
- **No dead code** - Remove unused imports, functions, files immediately
- **Self-documenting** - Code explains "what", comments explain "why"

#### 7. 📈 RETENTION (Bring Users Back)
- **Valuable data** - Help users track stats they can't get elsewhere
- **Progress visualization** - Show improvement over time
- **Social features** - Playing partners, comparisons, friendly competition
- **Low friction** - One-tap round logging, smart defaults, remember preferences
- **Notifications done right** - Valuable reminders, never spam

#### 8. ⚡ EFFICIENT + OPTIMIZED (Performance)
- **Measure first** - Profile before optimizing, no premature optimization
- **Performance budgets** - Set and enforce bundle size limits
- **Core Web Vitals** - LCP < 2.5s, FID < 100ms, CLS < 0.1
- **Database optimization** - Proper indexes, query analysis, N+1 prevention
- **Caching strategy** - Cache at every layer (CDN, API, database, client)
- **Lazy loading** - Load code and data only when needed

#### 9. 🔄 UPDATED + KNOWLEDGEABLE (Stay Current)
- **Current dependencies** - Regular updates, security patches applied promptly
- **Modern patterns** - Use current best practices, avoid deprecated approaches
- **Understand before changing** - Read existing code thoroughly before modifying
- **Know the "why"** - Understand reasoning behind architectural decisions
- **Documentation current** - CLAUDE.md and docs updated with every change

#### 10. 🏌️ INDUSTRY-FAMILIAR (Golf Domain Expertise)
- **Handicap systems** - GHIN, WHS, course/slope ratings, handicap index calculation
- **Golf terminology** - Correct use of GIR, FIR, putts per round, scoring terms
- **Course data** - Par, rating, slope, tee boxes, layouts
- **Tournament formats** - Stroke play, match play, Stableford, scramble
- **Competitor awareness** - Know what GHIN, Golf Pad, 18Birdies offer

---

### Golden Rules (Never Break)

1. **CSV is source of truth** - Never edit JSON in `/data/` directly
2. **Always run `npx prisma generate`** after schema.prisma changes
3. **Protected routes need `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard)`**
4. **Admin routes need `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard, AdminGuard)`**
5. **All inputs validated** via class-validator DTOs
6. **Never commit secrets** - Check `.env`, API keys, tokens
7. **Test before deploy** - No untested code reaches production
8. **Mobile-first** - Every feature must work on mobile devices

### Before Completing ANY Task

- [ ] **Security:** No vulnerabilities introduced (injection, XSS, auth bypass)
- [ ] **Stability:** Error handling for all failure modes
- [ ] **UX:** Works on mobile, accessible, has loading/error states
- [ ] **Lean:** No unnecessary code, dependencies, or features added
- [ ] **Clean:** Follows existing patterns, readable, no dead code
- [ ] **Tests:** Critical paths have test coverage
- [ ] **Backend:** `npm run test` passes
- [ ] **Frontend:** `npm run build` passes, `npm run check:data` passes
- [ ] **Docs:** CLAUDE.md updated if patterns changed

### CSV Data Workflow

```bash
# 1. Edit CSV file
vim golfers/golfers.csv

# 2. Regenerate JSON
npm run build:data

# 3. Verify
npm run check:data

# 4. Commit BOTH files
git add golfers/golfers.csv data/golfers.json
git commit -m "data(golfers): update..."
```

---

## Architecture Overview

### Frontend Data Flow

```
CSV files (source of truth)     JSON (build artifact)       Browser
golfers/golfers.csv      -->    data/golfers.json     -->   fetch + render
leagues/leagues.csv      -->    data/leagues.json
tours/tours.csv          -->    data/tours.json
```

### Backend Module Structure

```
src/
├── auth/           # Google OAuth + JWT (cookie: lg_jwt)
├── profile/        # GET/PUT /v1/me
├── rounds/         # CRUD /v1/rounds
├── courses/        # Course management
├── prisma/         # Database service (global module)
└── audit-log/      # Action logging
```

### Database Models

| Model | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `User` | OAuth identity (Google), 1:1 with Player |
| `Player` | Golf profile (handicap, GHIN), has many Rounds |
| `Round` | Golf rounds with stats |
| `Course` | Course data with API integration |
| `RoundLink` | Playing partner linking |
| `Role/UserRole` | RBAC |
| `AuditLog` | Action tracking |

### Authentication Flow

1. Frontend → `GET /auth/google` → Google consent
2. Google → Backend callback with code
3. Backend creates/updates User + Player
4. Backend signs JWT, sets `lg_jwt` HttpOnly cookie
5. Backend redirects to frontend success page
6. Frontend calls `/auth/session` to confirm

---

## Environment Variables

### Backend (.env)

```ini
PORT=3000
NODE_ENV=development|production
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:pass@localhost:5432/lazygolfing
JWT_SECRET=<openssl rand -base64 32>
GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=<from-google-console>
GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=<from-google-console>
GOOGLE_CALLBACK_URL=http://localhost:3000/auth/google/redirect
```

### CORS Origins (src/main.ts)

```
https://lazygolfing.com
https://www.lazygolfing.com
https://lazygolfing-com.pages.dev
```

For local development, add your origin (e.g., `http://localhost:8080`).

---

## Troubleshooting Quick Reference

| Symptom | Cause | Solution |
|---------|-------|----------|
| Table not rendering | JSON not loaded | Check Network tab for 404 |
| Stale data | Browser cache | Hard refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R) |
| CSV changes not showing | Forgot build | `npm run build:data` |
| `Prisma Client not found` | Schema changed | `npx prisma generate` |
| 401 Unauthorized | Invalid JWT | Re-login |
| CORS error | Origin not allowed | Add to `main.ts` allowlist |

### Planning Resources

| [Product Roadmap](./docs/plans/PRODUCT_ROADMAP.md) | Vision, timeline, success metrics |
| [Feature Roadmap](./docs/plans/FEATURE_ROADMAP.md) | Comprehensive feature list |

### Project-Specific Guides

| Document | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [Frontend CLAUDE.md](./lzg.home/CLAUDE.md) | Next.js 15, React 19, components |
| [Backend CLAUDE.md](./lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md) | NestJS 11, Prisma, authentication |

---

## AI Working Principles

### Approach

1. **Read first, act later** - Understand existing code before modifying
2. **Follow patterns** - Match the style of surrounding code
3. **Test before completing** - Run tests, verify manually
4. **Security first** - Never expose secrets, validate inputs

### When to Ask

- Requirements are ambiguous
- Security implications unclear
- Breaking changes needed
- Performance trade-offs exist

### When to Proceed

- Implementation matches existing patterns
- Non-breaking additions
- Bug fixes with clear root cause
- Refactoring that preserves behavior

---

## Brand Guidelines

### Voice
- Tagline: "Lazy golf for lazy golfers!" + "Cart Golf Only!"
- Friendly, inclusive, self-aware
- Celebrate small wins and funny losses

### Miami Vice Color Palette

| Color | Hex | Theme Usage |
|-------|-----|-------------|
| Hot Pink | #ff0080 | Dark mode primary |
| Cyan | #00f0ff | Dark mode secondary |
| Purple | #9b59ff | Dark mode accent |
| Coral | #ff6b6b | Light mode primary |
| Orange | #ff7033 | Light mode secondary |
| Yellow | #ffbf00 | Light mode accent |

**Single source of truth:** `lzg.home/src/lib/theme.ts`

See [Design System](./docs/design/DESIGN_INSPIRATION.md) for full documentation.

---

## Workspace Organization Rules

These rules apply across all repositories in the Lazy Golfing workspace.

### Single Source of Truth Principle

Every piece of data/configuration should have ONE authoritative source:

| What | Source of Truth | Don't Duplicate In |
|------|----------------|-------------------|
| Brand colors | `lzg.home/src/lib/theme.ts` | Individual CSS files |
| SEO metadata | `lzg.home/src/lib/metadata.ts` | Individual page files |
| API types | `lzg.backend/src/` DTOs | Frontend type files |
| CSV data | `public/alpha/*.csv` files | JSON directly |

### Static Asset Organization

All static assets MUST be organized in appropriate folders:

```
public/
├── favicons/     # All favicon files
├── images/       # Logos, OG images, photos
├── patterns/     # SVG patterns, textures
└── fonts/        # Custom fonts (if any)
```

**Never place files loose in public/ root** (except site.webmanifest, robots.txt).

| OG Title | All lowercase for brand ("lazy golfing") |
| OG Description | Tagline: "lazy golf for lazy golfers!" |
| Safe Zone | Center 800x400 area for cropping |

### Documentation Hierarchy

```
/CLAUDE.md                    # Quick reference, critical rules

  ├── plans/                  # Roadmaps, feature lists

  ├── technical-debt/         # Code quality tracking

/lzg.home/CLAUDE.md          # Frontend-specific rules

# CLAUDE.md - Frontend (lzg.home) > AI assistant guidelines for the Next.js frontend application **Last Updated:** 2025-12-06 **Version:** 2.0 (Next.js migration) --- ## ⚠️ Critical Priorities (Frontend Application) > **See [/CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md) for the complete priority hierarchy.** This section covers frontend-specific applications of those principles. ### Frontend Priority Applications | Priority | Frontend Implementation | |----------|------------------------| | 🔒 **Security** | XSS prevention (never inject raw HTML), CSRF tokens on mutations, no secrets in client bundle, sanitize user content with DOMPurify | | 🏗️ **Foundational** | Single layout source (no Header/Footer in pages), centralized config (`theme.ts`, `metadata.ts`), consistent component patterns | | 🛡️ **Stability** | Error boundaries on route segments, loading states for async, fallback UI for failed fetches, null checks everywhere | | ✨ **UX/UI** | <100ms interactions, skeleton loaders, optimistic updates, mobile-first responsive, WCAG 2.1 AA, Miami Vice theme consistency | | 🎯 **Lean** | Tree-shake imports, lazy load routes, question every npm package, no unused components | | 🧹 **Clean** | Components <200 lines, clear prop types, barrel exports for directories, no inline styles | | ⚡ **Performance** | Bundle size <200KB initial, images optimized, fonts preloaded, Core Web Vitals green | ### Frontend Checklist (Every Change) - [ ] Works on mobile (test at 375px width) - [ ] Has loading state for async operations - [ ] Has error state for failed operations - [ ] Follows existing component patterns - [ ] No `any` types (use proper TypeScript) - [ ] Accessible (keyboard nav, screen reader) - [ ] `npm run build` succeeds - [ ] No console errors/warnings --- ## SEO & Metadata (Single Source of Truth) ### Centralized Configuration All SEO metadata is defined in `src/lib/metadata.ts`: ```typescript import { createMetadata } from '@/lib/metadata'; // In any page: export const metadata = createMetadata({ title: 'Rounds', description: 'Track and view your golf rounds', path: '/rounds', }); ``` ### What's Included (Automatic) | Feature | Implementation | Location | |---------|---------------|----------| | **OpenGraph** | og:title, og:description, og:image | `metadata.ts` | | **Twitter Cards** | summary_large_image | `metadata.ts` | | **Schema.org** | WebSite, Organization schemas | `JsonLd` component | | **Favicon** | All sizes, apple-touch-icon | `metadata.ts` | | **Robots** | index, follow, googlebot | `metadata.ts` | | **Canonical URLs** | Per-page via path | `createMetadata()` | ### Schema.org Structured Data ```tsx // In layout.tsx or pages: import { JsonLd, websiteSchema, organizationSchema } from '@/components/seo'; // For dynamic pages (e.g., course details): import { createGolfCourseSchema } from '@/components/seo'; ``` ### Updating SEO Across the Platform | To Change | Edit This File | |-----------|---------------| | Site name, tagline, description | `src/lib/metadata.ts` → `siteConfig` | | OG image | Replace `/public/images/og-image.jpg` (1200x630) | | Favicons | Replace files in `/public/favicons/`, update `metadata.ts` icons config | | PWA manifest | Edit `/public/site.webmanifest` | | Twitter handle | `src/lib/metadata.ts` → `siteConfig.twitterHandle` | | Schema.org data | `src/components/seo/JsonLd.tsx` | | Page-specific meta | Export `metadata` from the page file | --- ## Public Folder Organization (Critical Rules) **RULE:** All static assets MUST be organized in appropriate subfolders. ### Folder Structure ``` public/ ├── favicons/ # All favicon-related files │ ├── favicon.ico # Classic favicon │ ├── favicon.svg # Scalable SVG favicon │ ├── favicon-96x96.png # High-res PNG favicon │ ├── favicon-32x32.png # Standard PNG favicon │ ├── favicon-16x16.png # Small PNG favicon │ ├── apple-touch-icon.png # iOS home screen (180x180) │ ├── web-app-manifest-192x192.png # PWA icon │ └── web-app-manifest-512x512.png # PWA icon large ├── images/ # Photos, logos, OG images │ ├── logo.png │ └── og-image.jpg # 1200x630 for social sharing ├── patterns/ # SVG patterns and textures │ └── grid.svg ├── alpha/ # Legacy site (don't modify) └── site.webmanifest # PWA manifest (references /favicons/) ``` ### Asset Path Rules | Asset Type | Folder | Example Path | |-----------|--------|--------------| | Favicons | `/favicons/` | `/favicons/favicon-32x32.png` | | Logos/Brand | `/images/` | `/images/logo.png` | | OG/Social Images | `/images/` | `/images/og-image.jpg` | | Photos/Screenshots | `/images/` | `/images/screenshot-1.png` | | SVG Patterns | `/patterns/` | `/patterns/grid.svg` | ### Social Sharing Image Specs | Platform | Optimal Size | Notes | |----------|-------------|-------| | OpenGraph (FB, LinkedIn, Discord) | 1200x630 | 1.91:1 ratio | | Twitter Cards | 1200x600 | 2:1 ratio (1200x630 works) | | Keep safe zone | 800x400 center | For cropping variations | ### Never Do This ```tsx // ❌ WRONG - Files loose in public/ src="/logo.png" src="/og-image.jpg" src="/grid.svg" // ✅ CORRECT - Files in organized folders src="/images/logo.png" src="/images/og-image.jpg" src="/patterns/grid.svg" ``` --- ## Miami Vice Theming System ### Centralized Color Configuration All colors are defined in `src/lib/theme.ts`: ```typescript // Full brand palette export const brandColors = { pink: { hex: '#ff0080', hsl: '330 100% 50%' }, cyan: { hex: '#00f0ff', hsl: '184 100% 50%' }, purple: { hex: '#9b59ff', hsl: '270 70% 60%' }, orange: { hex: '#ff7033', hsl: '25 100% 55%' }, yellow: { hex: '#ffbf00', hsl: '45 100% 55%' }, coral: { hex: '#ff6b6b', hsl: '10 100% 65%' }, }; ``` ### Theme-Adaptive Colors (globals.css) ```css :root { /* Light theme uses warmer colors */ --brand-primary: 25 100% 55%; /* Orange */ --brand-secondary: 45 100% 55%; /* Yellow */ --brand-accent: 10 100% 65%; /* Coral */ } .dark { /* Dark theme uses neon colors */ --brand-primary: 330 100% 55%; /* Hot Pink */ --brand-secondary: 184 100% 50%; /* Cyan */ --brand-accent: 270 70% 60%; /* Purple */ } ``` ### Using Theme Colors ```tsx // Theme-adaptive (changes with light/dark):
// Uses --brand-primary → --brand-secondary // Static brand colors (always same): // #ff0080 // #00f0ff
// Pink → Cyan
// Orange → Coral → Pink // Utility classes:
// Text glow effect
// Glass morphism
// Background glow effects ``` ### Theme Toggle ```tsx import { ThemeToggle } from '@/components/theme-toggle'; // In header: // Renders sun/moon icon button ``` --- ## Adding shadcn/ui Components ```bash # Add a component npx shadcn-ui@latest add button npx shadcn-ui@latest add card npx shadcn-ui@latest add dialog # Components go to src/components/ui/ ``` ### Available Components (pre-installed) - `Button` - Primary UI button - Additional components added as needed ### Usage Pattern ```tsx import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'; ``` --- ## API Integration ### API Client (`src/lib/api.ts`) ```typescript import { apiRequest, API_BASE, IS_DEV } from '@/lib/api'; // GET request const { ok, data } = await apiRequest('/v1/endpoint'); // POST with body const result = await apiRequest('/v1/rounds', { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify({ courseName: 'Test', strokes: 85 }), }); // Response type interface ApiResponse { ok: boolean; status: number; data: T | null; } ``` **Features:** - Auto-detects localhost vs production - Includes credentials (for JWT cookies) - CSRF token extraction from cookies - Handles 204 No Content responses ### Authentication (`src/lib/auth.ts`) ```typescript import { getSession, getMe, logout, getGoogleLoginUrl } from '@/lib/auth'; // Check auth status const session = await getSession(); if (session.authenticated) { console.log(session.user.email); } // Get user profile const { ok, data: profile } = await getMe(); // Redirect to Google OAuth window.location.href = getGoogleLoginUrl(); ``` ### Environment Variables | Variable | Purpose | Default | |----------|---------|---------| | `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` | Backend API URL | Auto-detects based on hostname | **Auto-detection logic:** - If `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` is set → use that - If hostname is `localhost` or `127.0.0.1` → use `http://localhost:3000` - Otherwise → use `https://api.lazygolfing.com` **Development `.env.local`:** ``` NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://192.168.1.40:3000 ``` --- ## Routing ### App Router Structure ``` src/app/ ├── layout.tsx # Root layout (Header, Footer, Schema) ├── page.tsx # / (home) - content only ├── about/page.tsx # /about ├── (auth)/ # Auth route group (minimal header) │ ├── layout.tsx # Auth-specific layout │ └── login/page.tsx # /login ├── (main)/ # Future: Pages with full nav │ ├── rounds/ │ │ ├── page.tsx # /rounds (list) │ │ └── [id]/page.tsx # /rounds/123 (detail) │ ├── leagues/ │ │ └── page.tsx # /leagues │ ├── tournaments/ │ │ └── page.tsx # /tournaments │ └── profile/ │ └── page.tsx # /profile ``` ### Route Groups Explained | Group | Layout | Use Case | |-------|--------|----------| | `(auth)` | Minimal header, no nav | Login, signup, forgot password | | `(main)` | Full header/footer | Most public pages | | `(dashboard)` | Sidebar + header | Authenticated user pages | ### Link to Legacy Site ```tsx // Link to old pages during migration: Sign In (Legacy) ``` --- ## Cloudflare Pages Deployment ### Build Settings - **Build command:** `npm run build` - **Build output directory:** `out` - **Root directory:** `lzg.home` ### Static Export The `next.config.ts` is configured for static export: ```typescript const nextConfig = { output: 'export', // Static HTML export trailingSlash: true, // /about/ instead of /about images: { unoptimized: true }, // Required for static }; ``` ### Output Structure ``` out/ ├── index.html # New home page ├── rounds/index.html # New rounds page ├── alpha/ # Legacy site (copied from public/) │ ├── index.html │ └── ... └── _next/ # JS/CSS bundles ``` --- ## Development Workflow ### Starting Development ```bash cd lzg.home npm install npm run dev ``` ### Creating a New Page ```tsx // src/app/courses/page.tsx import { createMetadata } from '@/lib/metadata'; // 1. Add page-specific metadata export const metadata = createMetadata({ title: 'Courses', description: 'Browse golf courses and view ratings, slopes, and reviews.', path: '/courses', }); // 2. Page is content-only (Header/Footer come from layout.tsx) export default function CoursesPage() { return (

Courses

{/* Content here */}
); } ``` **Checklist for new pages:** - [ ] Export `metadata` using `createMetadata()` for SEO - [ ] Don't include Header/Footer (they're in layout.tsx) - [ ] Use the standard content wrapper pattern - [ ] Add to navigation in `components/layout/Header.tsx` if needed ### Creating a Component ```tsx // src/components/course-card.tsx interface CourseCardProps { name: string; location: string; par: number; } export function CourseCard({ name, location, par }: CourseCardProps) { return (

{name}

{location}

Par {par}

); } ``` --- ## Styling Guidelines ### Use Tailwind Classes ```tsx // Good - Tailwind utility classes
// Avoid - inline styles
``` ### CSS Variables for Theming ```tsx // Good - uses theme variables
// Avoid - hardcoded colors
``` ### Responsive Design ```tsx // Mobile-first approach
``` --- ## Legacy Site (Alpha) The previous static site is preserved at `/alpha/`: - **URL:** https://lazygolfing.com/alpha/ - **Location:** `public/alpha/` - **Purpose:** Fallback during migration ### Legacy Files ``` public/alpha/ ├── index.html, profile.html, rounds.html, ... ├── js/auth.js # Authentication client ├── js/profile.js, rounds.js # Page logic ├── css/style.css # Custom styles ├── data/ # Generated JSON ├── golfers/, leagues/, tours/ # CSV + scripts └── scripts/convert-csv.js # Data pipeline ``` ### Maintaining Legacy Data The CSV → JSON pipeline still works: ```bash npm run build:data # Converts CSVs in public/alpha/ npm run check:data # Verifies JSON is current ``` --- ## File Naming Conventions | Type | Convention | Example | |------|------------|---------| | Components | PascalCase | `CourseCard.tsx` | | Pages | kebab-case folders | `src/app/my-rounds/page.tsx` | | Utilities | camelCase | `utils.ts`, `apiClient.ts` | | Hooks | use prefix | `useAuth.ts`, `useRounds.ts` | | Types | PascalCase | `types.ts` → `export type Round` | --- ## Common Patterns ### Client Components ```tsx 'use client'; // Add at top for interactivity import { useState } from 'react'; export function Counter() { const [count, setCount] = useState(0); return ; } ``` ### Server Components (Default) ```tsx // No 'use client' - runs on server export default async function Page() { const data = await fetch('https://api.example.com/data'); return
{/* render data */}
; } ``` ### Loading States ```tsx // src/app/rounds/loading.tsx export default function Loading() { return
Loading...
; } ``` --- ## Debugging ### Check Theme ```javascript // Browser console document.documentElement.classList.contains('dark') // true if dark mode ``` ### Check API Base ```javascript // Environment variable in browser console.log(process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL); ``` ### Common Issues | Symptom | Cause | Solution | |---------|-------|----------| | Hydration mismatch | Theme flash | `suppressHydrationWarning` on html | | 404 on routes | Static export | Check `trailingSlash: true` | | Styles not loading | Tailwind purge | Check `content` in tailwind.config | | API CORS errors | Wrong origin | Add localhost to backend CORS | | Build fails | Type errors | Run `npm run lint` first | --- ## Related Documentation | Document | Description | |----------|-------------| | [/CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md) | Root monorepo overview | | [/lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md](../lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md) | Backend API documentation | | [/docs/architecture/API_REFERENCE.md](../docs/architecture/API_REFERENCE.md) | Complete API endpoint reference | | [/docs/architecture/DATABASE_SCHEMA.md](../docs/architecture/DATABASE_SCHEMA.md) | Prisma schema & ER diagram | | [/docs/architecture/SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md](../docs/architecture/SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md) | High-level architecture | | [/docs/design/DESIGN_INSPIRATION.md](../docs/design/DESIGN_INSPIRATION.md) | Miami Vice design system | | [/docs/plans/FEATURE_ROADMAP.md](../docs/plans/FEATURE_ROADMAP.md) | Comprehensive feature list | --- *This file was updated during the Next.js v2.0 + Miami Vice migration (2025-12-06).* *Architecture patterns (Layout, SEO, Route Groups) added 2025-12-06.*

    13→### Backend Priority Applications

    18→| 🏗️ **Foundational** | Prisma as single DB interface, NestJS module structure, consistent error responses, proper database constraints/indexes |
    19→| 🛡️ **Stability** | Try-catch on all async, transaction rollbacks, connection pooling, graceful shutdown, health checks |

    21→| 🎯 **Lean** | No unused endpoints, minimal dependencies, efficient queries (avoid N+1), lazy load relations |
    22→| 🧹 **Clean** | Service/Controller separation, DTOs for all inputs, consistent naming, typed Prisma queries |
    23→| ⚡ **Performance** | Database indexes on query fields, query optimization, connection pooling, response caching where safe |

   203→## Authentication Flow

   216→
   217→```typescript
   218→{

   246→## CSRF Protection
   247→
   248→### Double-Submit Cookie Pattern
   249→
   250→Protected endpoints require the `X-CSRF-Token` header to match the `csrf_token` cookie.
   251→
   252→**Cookie:** Set at login alongside `lg_jwt`
   253→**Header:** `X-CSRF-Token: <token-value>`
   254→**Validation:** Constant-time comparison (prevents timing attacks)
   255→
   256→```typescript
   257→// Frontend request example
   258→fetch('/v1/me', {
   259→  credentials: 'include',  // Include cookies
   260→  headers: {
   261→    'X-CSRF-Token': getCookie('csrf_token')
   262→  }
   263→});
   264→```
   265→
   266→**Skip for:** GET, HEAD, OPTIONS requests

# CLAUDE.md - Backend (lzg.backend)

> AI assistant guidelines for the NestJS API backend

**Last Updated:** 2025-12-06

---

## ⚠️ Critical Priorities (Backend API)

> **See [/CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md) for the complete priority hierarchy.** This section covers backend-specific applications of those principles.

### Backend Priority Applications

| Priority | Backend Implementation |
|----------|----------------------|
| 🔒 **Security** | JWT + CSRF on all mutations, input validation via DTOs, parameterized queries (Prisma), rate limiting, never log secrets, RBAC with AdminGuard |
| 🏗️ **Foundational** | Prisma as single DB interface, NestJS module structure, consistent error responses, proper database constraints/indexes |
| 🛡️ **Stability** | Try-catch on all async, transaction rollbacks, connection pooling, graceful shutdown, health checks |
| ✨ **UX/UI** | Fast API responses (<200ms), meaningful error messages, proper HTTP status codes, pagination on lists |
| 🎯 **Lean** | No unused endpoints, minimal dependencies, efficient queries (avoid N+1), lazy load relations |
| 🧹 **Clean** | Service/Controller separation, DTOs for all inputs, consistent naming, typed Prisma queries |
| ⚡ **Performance** | Database indexes on query fields, query optimization, connection pooling, response caching where safe |
| 📊 **Observable** | Audit logging for mutations, structured error logging, PM2 monitoring |

### Backend Checklist (Every Change)

- [ ] Protected routes have `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard)`
- [ ] Admin routes have `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard, AdminGuard)`
- [ ] All inputs validated via class-validator DTOs
- [ ] User can only access their own data (no IDOR)
- [ ] Sensitive data filtered from responses
- [ ] No secrets in code or logs
- [ ] Database queries are efficient (check for N+1)
- [ ] `npm run test` passes
- [ ] `npx prisma generate` run after schema changes

### Security Non-Negotiables

```typescript
// ✅ ALWAYS: Protected mutation endpoint
@Post()
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard)
async create(@Body() dto: CreateDto, @CurrentUser() user: User) {
  // Validate ownership in service layer
}

// ✅ ALWAYS: Admin-only endpoint
@Delete(':id')
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard, AdminGuard)
async delete(@Param('id') id: string) { }

// ❌ NEVER: Unprotected mutation
@Post()
async create(@Body() dto: CreateDto) { }  // NO GUARDS = SECURITY HOLE
```

---

## Quick Reference

### Environment

- **Code editing:** Windows machine (this repo)
- **Runtime:** Ubuntu VM at `192.168.1.40`
- **Production:** https://api.lazygolfing.com

### Essential Commands (Run on VM)

```bash
# Development
npm run start:dev               # Hot-reload server
npm run start:debug             # Debug mode (port 9229)
npm run test                    # Unit tests
npm run lint                    # ESLint

# Database
npx prisma generate             # REQUIRED after schema changes
npx prisma migrate dev --name <name>  # Create migration
npx prisma studio               # GUI at 192.168.1.40:5555

# Production
pm2 logs lg-api --lines 100     # View logs
pm2 restart lg-api              # After deploy
```

---

## Module Structure

```
src/
├── main.ts                     # Bootstrap (CORS, Helmet, validation, cookies)
├── app.module.ts               # Root module + ThrottlerModule
├── config/
│   └── env.validation.ts       # Environment variable validation
├── auth/                       # Google OAuth + JWT + CSRF
│   ├── auth.controller.ts      # /auth/google, /auth/session, /auth/logout
│   ├── auth.service.ts         # findOrCreateUser, validateUser, ensureRoles
│   ├── google.strategy.ts      # Passport Google OAuth
│   ├── jwt.strategy.ts         # Passport JWT validation
│   ├── jwt-auth.guard.ts       # Guard for protected routes
│   ├── admin.guard.ts          # Guard for admin-only routes
│   ├── csrf.guard.ts           # CSRF double-submit protection
│   ├── csrf.util.ts            # CSRF token generation
│   └── current-user.decorator.ts  # @CurrentUser() decorator
├── profile/                    # User profile + stats
│   ├── profile.controller.ts   # GET/PUT /v1/me, /v1/me/stats, /v1/me/export
│   ├── profile.service.ts      # getProfile, updateProfile, getStats, export
│   └── update-profile.dto.ts   # Validation DTO
├── rounds/                     # Golf rounds + linking
│   ├── rounds.controller.ts    # CRUD /v1/rounds + link endpoints
│   ├── rounds.service.ts       # Business logic + partner matching
│   └── dto/                    # create-round.dto.ts, update-round.dto.ts, import-csv.dto.ts
├── courses/                    # Course management + Golf API
│   ├── courses.controller.ts   # Search, nearby, CRUD
│   ├── courses.service.ts      # Search, API integration, Haversine
│   └── dto/                    # create-course.dto.ts, update-course.dto.ts
├── prisma/                     # Database (Global module)
│   └── prisma.service.ts       # Extends PrismaClient
└── audit-log/                  # Action logging
    ├── audit-log.module.ts     # Module definition
    └── audit-log.service.ts    # log(), getUserLogs(), cleanup()
```

## API Endpoints

### Authentication (Public)

| Method | Path | Guard | Description |
|--------|------|-------|-------------|
| GET | `/auth/google` | Google OAuth | Start OAuth flow |
| GET | `/auth/google/redirect` | Google OAuth | OAuth callback |
| GET | `/auth/session` | None | Check login status |
| POST | `/auth/logout` | CSRF | Clear session |

### Profile (Requires JWT + CSRF)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/v1/me` | Get user + player profile |
| PUT | `/v1/me` | Update player profile |
| GET | `/v1/me/stats` | Get user statistics |
| GET | `/v1/me/playing-partners` | Get aggregated partners |
| GET | `/v1/me/export` | GDPR data export |
| DELETE | `/v1/me` | Delete account (cascade) |

### Rounds (Requires JWT + CSRF)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/v1/rounds` | List player's rounds (paginated) |
| POST | `/v1/rounds` | Create round |
| PUT | `/v1/rounds/:id` | Update round |
| DELETE | `/v1/rounds/:id` | Soft delete round |
| POST | `/v1/rounds/import-csv` | Bulk import rounds |
| GET | `/v1/rounds/:id/suggestions` | Find playing partner matches |
| POST | `/v1/rounds/:id/link/:targetId` | Link two rounds |
| GET | `/v1/rounds/:id/linked` | Get linked rounds |

### Courses

| Method | Path | Guard | Description |
|--------|------|-------|-------------|
| GET | `/v1/courses/search` | None | Search by name (+ API) |
| GET | `/v1/courses/nearby` | None | GPS-based search |
| GET | `/v1/courses` | None | List all courses |
| GET | `/v1/courses/:id` | None | Course details + stats |
| POST | `/v1/courses` | JWT+CSRF | Create course |
| POST | `/v1/courses/import` | JWT+CSRF | Import from Golf API |
| PUT | `/v1/courses/:id` | JWT+CSRF+Admin | Update course |
| DELETE | `/v1/courses/:id` | JWT+CSRF+Admin | Delete course |

---

## Database Schema

### Model Relationships

```
User (OAuth identity)
  └── 1:1 Player (golf profile)
           └── 1:N Round
                     └── N:1 Course (optional)

Role ←─ N:N ─→ User (via UserRole)
AuditLog (standalone)
```

### Key Fields

| Model | Key Fields |
|-------|------------|
| User | email, provider, providerId, avatarUrl, lastLoginAt |
| Player | firstName, lastName, ghinNumber, handicap |
| Round | strokes, front9, back9, girPct, firPct, totalPutts, birdies, pars, bogeys |
| Course | name, city, state, totalPar, courseRating, slopeRating, apiCourseId |

---

## Authentication Flow

```
1. Frontend → GET /auth/google
2. Redirect to Google consent
3. Google → /auth/google/redirect with code
4. GoogleStrategy exchanges code for user info
5. auth.service.findOrCreateUser() → User + Player
6. Sign JWT, set lg_jwt cookie (HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=None, 7 days)
7. Redirect to frontend success page
```

### Cookie Config

```typescript
{
  httpOnly: true,        // XSS protection
  secure: true,          // HTTPS only (production)
  sameSite: 'none',      // Cross-origin allowed
  maxAge: 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000,  // 7 days
}
```

### Protecting Routes

```typescript
// Basic JWT protection
@Get('protected-route')
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard)
async myRoute(@CurrentUser() user: any) {
  // user contains JWT payload: { sub: userId, email }
}

// Admin-only route
@Put('admin-route')
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard, AdminGuard)
async adminOnly(@CurrentUser() user: any) {
  // Only users with 'admin' role can access
}
```

---

## CSRF Protection

### Double-Submit Cookie Pattern

Protected endpoints require the `X-CSRF-Token` header to match the `csrf_token` cookie.

**Cookie:** Set at login alongside `lg_jwt`
**Header:** `X-CSRF-Token: <token-value>`
**Validation:** Constant-time comparison (prevents timing attacks)

```typescript
// Frontend request example
fetch('/v1/me', {
  credentials: 'include',  // Include cookies
  headers: {
    'X-CSRF-Token': getCookie('csrf_token')
  }
});
```

**Skip for:** GET, HEAD, OPTIONS requests

---

## Rate Limiting

Global rate limiting via `@nestjs/throttler`:

| Category | Limit | Window |
|----------|-------|--------|
| Default | 120 requests | 1 minute |
| Auth endpoints | 20 requests | 1 minute |
| Session check | 20 requests | 1 minute |

**Response when exceeded:** `429 Too Many Requests`

```typescript
// 1. Add DTO (src/[module]/dto/[action].dto.ts)
export class CreateThingDto {
  @IsString()
  @IsNotEmpty()
  name: string;
}

// 2. Add service method
async createThing(playerId: string, dto: CreateThingDto) {
  return this.prisma.thing.create({
    data: { ...dto, playerId },
  });
}

// 3. Add controller route
@Post()
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)
async create(
  @Body() dto: CreateThingDto,
  @CurrentUser() user: any,
) {
  const profile = await this.profileService.getProfile(user.id);
  return this.thingService.createThing(profile.player.id, dto);
}

// 4. Write tests and run
npm run test
```

# CLAUDE.md Compliance Checklist

**Purpose:** Ensure every development task follows the mandatory CLAUDE.md workflow
**Usage:** Reference this checklist BEFORE starting ANY coding task

---

## Pre-Flight Checklist

### Before Starting ANY Task

- [ ] **Read CLAUDE.md** (especially rules #1-11)
- [ ] **Sync with main branch:** `git fetch origin main && git merge origin/main --no-edit`
- [ ] **Create task file:** `tasks/[feature-name].md` using template from `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
- [ ] **Fill out task plan:**
  - Problem statement
  - Files to read
  - Proposed changes
  - Todo checklist with estimates
- [ ] **Get user approval** via `ExitPlanMode` tool (Rule #3)
- [ ] **ONLY THEN** begin implementation

---

## During Implementation

### As You Work

- [ ] **Update todos** as you complete each item (Rule #2)
- [ ] **Make changes as simple as possible** (Rule #9)
  - Impact minimal code
  - Avoid complex refactors unless necessary
  - Keep changes focused on the task
- [ ] **Give high-level explanations** of changes (Rule #5)
- [ ] **No temporary fixes** - Find and fix root causes (Rule #8)
- [ ] **Follow privacy/security/UX principles** (CLAUDE.md Core Principles)

---

## When Complete

### Before Marking Work "Done"

- [ ] **Add review section** to `tasks/[feature].md` (Rule #7)
  - Summary of changes
  - Files created/modified
  - Testing status
  - Issues encountered
  - Time comparison (estimated vs actual)
- [ ] **Update documentation** if architecture changed (Rule #10)
  - `docs/technical/` for new systems/patterns
  - `docs/product/feature_map.md` for new features
  - Any other relevant docs
- [ ] **Run tests:** `npm run test`
- [ ] **Type check:** `npm run type-check`
- [ ] **Lint check:** `npm run lint`
- [ ] **Build check:** `npm run build` (verify bundle size < 100KB)
- [ ] **Verify in light mode**
- [ ] **Verify in dark mode**
- [ ] **Test keyboard navigation**
- [ ] **Test at different screen sizes** (375px, 768px, 1024px)

---

## Git Commit Checklist

### Before Creating Commit

- [ ] **Stage task file:** `git add tasks/[feature].md` (pre-commit hook will block if missing)
- [ ] **Review changes:** `git diff --staged`
- [ ] **Write clear commit message:**
  ```
  type: Short description (50 chars max)

  - Bullet point details
  - What changed and why
  - Related issues (#123)

  Generated with Claude Code
  Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
